name: Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop
name_original: Villar del Olmo a Ambite — circular por Encina de la Pica
description: This easy 9.4-mile mountain biking loop near Villar del Olmo offers a quick ride past local landmarks and natural features.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villar Del Olmo offers diverse landscapes within the Community of Madrid, characterized by rolling mountains, green valleys, and varied natural environments. The region features a network of trails, including gravel paths and unpaved segments, suitable for different skill levels. It is part of the broader Sierra de Madrid, providing a picturesque backdrop of pine and oak forests, rocky peaks, and serene meadows. The Tajuña Greenway, a converted railway line, also provides distinct linear cycling routes. Best mountain bike trails around Villar Del Olmo The most popular mountain bike trail is Tajuña and 40 Days Train Greenway Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a historic railway line and offers varied scenery. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pezuela de las Torres — circular desde Nuevo Baztán, a moderate 22.0 miles (35.5 km) path. This route winds through rural landscapes and past historic villages like Nuevo Baztán. Local mountain bikers also love the Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through rolling hills and open fields, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Villar Del Olmo is defined by rolling mountains, green valleys, and extensive gravel paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Villar Del Olmo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villar Del Olmo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Villar Del Olmo?
A: Villar Del Olmo offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Villar Del Olmo?
A: The terrain around Villar Del Olmo is diverse, featuring rolling mountains, green valleys, and extensive gravel paths. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved segments, pine and oak forests, and open fields, providing varied scenery and riding experiences within the Sierra de Madrid.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Villar Del Olmo?
A: Yes, Villar Del Olmo has options for less experienced riders and families. There are 12 easy trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316451984/camino-de-la-pica-and-camino-del-arca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 15 km route through rolling hills and open fields, typically completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes.

Q: What are some popular moderate mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Many riders enjoy the moderate trails. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964010991/tajuna-and-40-days-train-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña and 40 Days Train Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.2 km route following a historic railway line. Another favorite is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316425724/pezuela-de-las-torres-circular-desde-nuevo-baztan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pezuela de las Torres — circular desde Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt;, a 35.5 km path winding through rural landscapes and historic villages.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 9 difficult trails around Villar Del Olmo. These routes often feature significant elevation gain and more technical sections, aligning with the Sierra de Madrid's reputation for technical singletracks and enduro riding.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I find along the trails?
A: The region offers several natural points of interest. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3255898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encina de la Pica&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3455613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Arca&lt;/a&gt;. These natural monuments and springs provide scenic spots along your ride.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in history. You can visit the beautiful historical complex of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt;, known for its cobbled streets and palace gardens. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219959" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of the Holy Children&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of cycling loops.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villar Del Olmo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villar Del Olmo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, picturesque landscapes, and the network of trails that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Villar Del Olmo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316425724/pezuela-de-las-torres-circular-desde-nuevo-baztan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pezuela de las Torres — circular desde Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1316451984/camino-de-la-pica-and-camino-del-arca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villar Del Olmo?
A: The Sierra de Madrid region, including Villar Del Olmo, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winter rides are possible but may require checking weather conditions for colder temperatures or occasional precipitation.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trailheads?
A: While specific parking areas for every trailhead are not detailed, Villar Del Olmo and its surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or route descriptions for suggested starting points and parking availability when planning your ride.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Villar Del Olmo is located within the Community of Madrid, and public transport options, primarily bus services, connect it to larger towns and the capital. While direct access to every trailhead by public transport might be limited, it is possible to reach the general area, and then cycle to your desired starting point.
tips: Villar del Olmo Town Hall has a QR code system, and you can discover the 21 points of interest, both scenic and historical, through your mobile or tablet, with information and videos from a drone view. There are many routes and many places of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is generally considered easy for mountain biking. While specific details for this exact route are not widely documented, similar 'Camino' trails often feature varied terrain, from relatively non-technical sections on jeep roads or paved paths to some sections with inclines. Given its easy rating, you can expect a mostly manageable ride.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the Villar del Olmo Town Center, and can also see the Monument to the Eyes. Natural features include the Fuente del Arca and the impressive Encina de la Pica.

faqs.question: Where does the Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for this loop starts and ends near Villar del Olmo. While specific parking information isn't detailed, it's common for trails originating from town centers like Villar del Olmo to have public parking options nearby. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is rated as easy, making it suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed mountain biking experience. The total elevation gain is around 250 meters over approximately 15 kilometers, which is manageable for most riders.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail would generally be during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the scenery is often at its most vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, and while winter riding is possible, you should check local weather conditions for potential rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this loop are not available. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Please be mindful of any posted signs regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to access this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required for the Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop. Most trails of this nature in the region are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino del Arca (19% of the route), the Camino de la Pica (16%), the Camino del Lanillo (3%), and the Travesía del Calvario (3%).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and passes through the Villar del Olmo Town Center, where you are likely to find local amenities such as cafes or small shops to refuel before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the loop?
faqs.answer: While specific wildlife for this exact trail isn't documented, trails in similar rural and natural settings often host common birds, small mammals, and various insects. Keep an eye out for local flora, especially during spring, which can add to the scenic beauty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Camino de la Pica and Camino del Arca Loop by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this 15-kilometer loop is approximately 1 hour and 17 minutes. This is an average time and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or points of interest.
